evals.prompt: I hand-rolled a little research agent in Python — it's in agent.py, just a loop that calls the OpenAI chat API and runs some tools, plus main.py (entry point) and tools.py. I want to see each turn, every model call, and every tool call show up in Weave's Agents tab. Can you wire up weave for me? My project is my-team/research-agent.
evals.expected_output: Conversation SDK instrumentation: weave.init at the entry point, the per-question loop wrapped in a Turn, each chat.completions.create wrapped in an LLM span, each tool dispatch wrapped in a Tool span. Behavior unchanged. User told to authenticate themselves. Handles/avoids the plain-openai auto-patch gotcha by using Conversation SDK spans.
expectations: weave is imported and weave.init("my-team/research-agent") is called exactly once at an entry point (main.py or module top), never inside the per-question loop
The per-question agent loop is wrapped in a Conversation SDK Turn (via weave.start_conversation/start_turn or conversation.start_turn)
Each OpenAI chat.completions.create call is wrapped in an LLM span (turn.start_llm / weave.start_llm) that records the model and output
Each tool execution is wrapped in a Tool span (turn.start_tool / weave.start_tool) capturing the tool name, arguments, and result
Spans are opened via context managers (with-blocks) so they close on exceptions, rather than bare start/end calls
run_agent still returns the final answer string and the original control flow and return values are unchanged
No W&B API key is hardcoded or written into source; the response instructs the user to authenticate in their own terminal (wandb login or WANDB_API_KEY)
The solution does not depend on plain `openai` being auto-traced (it uses Conversation SDK LLM spans), or it explicitly notes that plain openai is not auto-patched under use_otel_v2
All modified Python files are syntactically valid (parse without error)

evals.prompt: can you set up weave logging for this? it's an agent built on the openai agents sdk (agent.py). project name is my-team/support-bot
evals.expected_output: Auto-instrumentation path: add weave.init("my-team/support-bot") before the agent runs and rely on Weave's automatic instrumentation of the agent framework (here the OpenAI Agents SDK; the same auto path covers Claude Agent SDK and Google ADK, all landing in the Agents tab). Crucially, does NOT hand-wrap the code in Conversation SDK Turn/LLM/Tool spans. User told to authenticate themselves.
expectations: weave.init("my-team/support-bot") is added exactly once, before the agent is run

The solution does NOT add manual Conversation SDK spans (no Turn/LLM/Tool/SubAgent wrapping) around the auto-instrumented agent-framework code (the OpenAI Agents SDK here) — it relies on automatic instrumentation
The response explains that traces appear automatically (in the Agents tab) because Weave auto-instruments the agent framework (the OpenAI Agents SDK here)
Original agent behavior is unchanged (the Agent definition and Runner.run logic are intact)
No W&B API key is hardcoded; the user is told to authenticate themselves
All modified Python files are syntactically valid

evals.prompt: add weave tracing to this typescript agent (agent.ts + main.ts). i want the turns / llm calls / tool calls showing up as proper agent traces, not just flat call logs. project is my-team/ts-agent
evals.expected_output: TypeScript Conversation SDK instrumentation: await weave.init at the entry point, per-question loop wrapped in a Turn, model calls in LLM spans, tool executions in Tool spans, all via the start* functions and closed in finally blocks. Thrown errors are passed to end({error}) rather than merely closing success-looking spans. Behavior unchanged. User told to authenticate.
expectations: await weave.init("my-team/ts-agent") is called at the entry point (main.ts) before any traced work, and is awaited
Spans are created with the start* functions (weave.startConversation / startTurn / startLLM / startTool), NOT via `new Turn()` or direct class construction
The per-question loop body is wrapped in a Turn, each model call in an LLM span, and each tool execution in a Tool span
Every span is ended in a finally block (try/finally) so an exception cannot leak an open span, and caught thrown errors are passed to end({error}) so the exported status is not falsely successful
runAgent still returns the answer string and the original behavior is unchanged
No W&B API key is hardcoded; the user is told to authenticate (WANDB_API_KEY or weave.login)
The TypeScript imports weave correctly and uses real method/function names (no invented APIs)

evals.prompt: i've got a little python script (main.py) that calls the openai api a few times — i just want to see the calls, prompts and responses show up in weave. can you add that? project is my-team/qa-script
evals.expected_output: Handles the plain-openai default-mode gotcha: weave.init() ALONE does not trace plain `openai` under the default use_otel_v2=True (the import hook skips openai). Correct solution adds weave.init AND one of: weave.integrations.patch_openai() after init, WEAVE_USE_OTEL_V2=false in the run environment, or Conversation SDK LLM spans — and tells the user the calls land in the Calls tab. A solution that stops at weave.init() is wrong (silently zero traces).
expectations: weave is imported and weave.init("my-team/qa-script") is added exactly once at the entry point

The solution does NOT rely on weave.init() alone to trace plain `openai`: under the default use_otel_v2=True the import hook skips `openai`, so it must additionally do ONE of — call weave.integrations.patch_openai() after init, set WEAVE_USE_OTEL_V2=false in the run environment, or wrap the calls in Conversation SDK LLM spans
The response explicitly explains that plain `openai` is not auto-traced by weave.init() alone under the default settings, and states what was added to make the calls actually appear
The openai chat.completions.create calls and the script's control flow and printed output are unchanged
No W&B API key is hardcoded; the user is told to authenticate themselves (wandb login or WANDB_API_KEY)
All modified Python files are syntactically valid

evals.prompt: add weave to my node script so i can see the openai calls. it's an ESM project — entry is index.mjs, run with npm start. project my-team/esm-script
evals.expected_output: Handles the Node ESM auto-instrumentation gotcha: in an ESM project, importing 'weave' and calling weave.init() does NOT auto-patch plain `openai` — the process must be launched with `node --import=weave/instrument`. Correct solution adds weave + await weave.init AND updates the start command (package.json scripts.start) to include --import=weave/instrument (or explicitly wraps the calls). A solution that only adds weave.init() is wrong (no traces in ESM).
expectations: weave is added to package.json dependencies and imported in index.mjs, with await weave.init("my-team/esm-script") at the entry point before any traced work

Because this is an ESM project, the solution updates the run command (the package.json "start" script) to launch with `node --import=weave/instrument` so auto-instrumentation actually hooks — OR it explicitly wraps the openai calls; adding weave.init() alone is insufficient and must not be the whole fix
The response explains that ESM projects need the --import=weave/instrument preload flag (or explicit wrapping) for plain `openai` to be auto-traced
The openai chat.completions.create calls and the script's behavior and printed output are unchanged
No W&B API key is hardcoded; the user is told to authenticate (WANDB_API_KEY or weave.login)
index.mjs remains valid JavaScript (parses without error)

evals.prompt: we built our own little agent framework in-house (miniagent.py) and our app (app.py) uses it. i want proper agent traces — turns, llm calls, tool calls — in weave's agents tab. project my-team/inhouse-agent
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that the in-house `miniagent` framework is NOT a known Weave integration (it's not in the auto-instrumentation registry), so auto-instrumentation cannot produce agent traces for it. Falls back to the universal Conversation SDK: weave.init at the entry point, the agent's run cycle wrapped in a Turn, each model call in an LLM span, each tool dispatch in a Tool span — instrumenting the real boundaries inside miniagent.py. Does NOT claim weave.init alone will trace this framework. This is the case that proves the skill is generic for any agent, not a fixed framework list.
expectations: weave is imported and weave.init("my-team/inhouse-agent") is added exactly once at an entry point (app.py main or module top), never inside the agent loop

The solution uses the Conversation SDK (Turn/LLM/Tool via start_conversation/start_turn/start_llm/start_tool or the turn.* / conversation.* methods); it does NOT assume weave.init() alone auto-instruments the in-house `miniagent` framework

The agent's run cycle is wrapped in a Turn, each openai chat.completions.create call inside miniagent is wrapped in an LLM span, and each tool dispatch is wrapped in a Tool span — instrumenting the real boundaries even though they live inside miniagent.py
Spans are opened via context managers so they close on exceptions; the agent's return values and control flow are unchanged
No W&B API key is hardcoded; the user is told to authenticate themselves (wandb login or WANDB_API_KEY)
All modified Python files are syntactically valid, and every Weave API used is real (exists in the installed weave package)

evals.prompt: The Weave logging in agent.py was added during a wb_agent-style migration, and its mocked test passes, but the runtime traces are wrong. Repair the instrumentation without changing agent behavior. Preserve sharing_with_wandb_allowed, keep the tracing service account separate from the process's existing W&B identity, make the model chat and tool execution children of the same agent turn, and expose returned cancellation/error states. Replace or supplement the wiring-only test with an end-to-end span test that proves the logging behavior. Project is my-team/support-agent.
evals.expected_output: Repairs all runtime boundaries rather than moving wrappers around: denied requests emit no spans; initialization/auth are startup-scoped and do not overwrite the process W&B identity; the actual model request runs inside a child LLM/chat span and records complete request/completion metadata rather than output text alone; the actual tool dispatch runs inside a Tool/execute_tool span so duration and failures are truthful; returned terminal states are recorded before the turn closes. Adds an in-memory-exporter end-to-end regression asserting exact topology and payloads, including negative controls. A green mock asserting weave.init/patch calls alone is explicitly insufficient.
expectations: sharing_with_wandb_allowed gates the complete trace tree before a Conversation/Turn/model/tool span or patched provider request can be exported; the denied-path regression asserts zero exported spans and no prompt, output, argument, or result content

The dedicated tracing_api_key is not assigned permanently to process-wide WANDB_API_KEY and is never printed or hardcoded; any temporary environment override is limited to initialization and restored, or a dedicated exporter Authorization header is used

The real chat.completions.create call executes while a Conversation SDK LLM span is active, or a framework-native OTel patcher is used; the solution does not treat generic patch_openai Calls logging as proof of an Agents-tab child chat span, and does not treat llm.output(text) alone as a complete model payload
The end-to-end test asserts the chat span's parent span id equals the invoke_agent turn span id and both have the same trace id, rather than merely asserting that both spans exist

The Tool span encloses the live lookup_account dispatch rather than replaying result.tool_records after runner.run; a sleeping or raising tool proves truthful duration and error behavior while preserving the original result or exception

Returned completed, errored, and cancelled states are recorded with stable attributes before the turn closes; the solution does not rely on a context manager to infer failures represented as return data

The regression test inspects complete exported span payloads (operation names, parent/trace ids, exact input/output messages, response identity/model, usage/error/outcome fields) through an in-memory OTel exporter; tests that only mock weave.init or patch_openai do not satisfy this expectation
weave.init and authentication are moved out of the per-turn hot path without changing Runner outputs, exceptions, cancellation behavior, or tool behavior
All modified Python files are syntactically valid and every Weave API/import used exists in the installed package
